Protein names: L-carnitine:corrinoid methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.383) (Cobalamin-dependent carnitine demethylase) (L-carnitine methyltransferase) (L-carnitine--corrinoid protein Co-methyltransferase)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the trimethylamine methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=Co(I)-[quaternary-amine-specific corrinoid protein] + (R)-carnitine + H(+) = (3R)-4-(dimethylamino)-3-hydroxybutanoate + methyl-Co(III)-[quaternary-amine-specific corrinoid protein]; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:69384, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:17694, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:17695, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:16347, ChEBI:CHEBI:85033, ChEBI:CHEBI:85035, ChEBI:CHEBI:183234; EC=2.1.1.383; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:32571881}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:69385; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:32571881};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the degradation of the quaternary amine L-carnitine (PubMed:32571881). Component of a corrinoid-dependent methyltransferase system that transfers a methyl group from L-carnitine to tetrahydrofolate (THF), forming methyl-THF, a key intermediate in the Wood-Ljungdahl acetogenesis pathway (PubMed:32571881). MtcB catalyzes the methylation of the corrinoid protein MtqC, using L-carnitine as the methyl donor (PubMed:32571881). L-carnitine demethylation generates the unusual biological product norcarnitine, which is likely degraded by other members of the gut microbiota (PubMed:32571881). In vitro, can methylate free cob(I)alamin (PubMed:32571881). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:32571881}.

Protein names: Glycine betaine methyltransferase (GB methyltransferase) (EC 2.1.1.376) (Glycine betaine--corrinoid protein Co-methyltransferase) (Glycine betaine:cob(I)alamin methyltransferase) (Glycine betaine:corrinoid methyltransferase)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the trimethylamine methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=Co(I)-[glycine betaine-specific corrinoid protein] + glycine betaine + H(+) = methyl-Co(III)-[glycine betaine-specific corrinoid protein] + N,N-dimethylglycine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:65996, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:16947, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:16948, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:17750, ChEBI:CHEBI:58251, ChEBI:CHEBI:85033, ChEBI:CHEBI:85035; EC=2.1.1.376; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:25313086};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Methyltransferase able to methylate free cob(I)alamin in vitro, using glycine betaine as the methyl donor, yealding methylcobalamin (methylCbl) and dimethylglycine. In vivo, probably carries out the methylation of a corrinoid protein, likely the adjacently encoded DSY3155, with glycine betaine, to then supply methyl groups to tetrahydrofolate (THF) for ultimate conversion to carbon dioxide; oxidation of the methyl group would also provide reducing equivalents for anaerobic respiration. Thus, may function in the pathway that allows anaerobic methylotrophic growth of D.hafniense using glycine betaine. Cannot use quaternary amines such as carnitine and choline as substrates, nor tertiary amines such as dimethylglycine or trimethylamine. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:25313086}.
